text.title: A/B Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every A/B Testing application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The primary metric MUST be declared before the test starts. Post-hoc primary-metric switching is rejected.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc primary switching guarantees PROCEED somewhere; pre-declaration is the only defence against p-hacking.

rule.statement: Sample size MUST be computed before the test starts (baseline rate, MDE, power ≥0.8, alpha ≤0.05). Under-powered tests are rejected.
rule.rationale: Under-powered tests produce noise; ending early on a "winner" with insufficient sample is the most common A/B failure.

rule.statement: Guardrail metrics MUST be declared and watched. A guardrail regression (>threshold) overrides the primary win and forces KILL.
rule.rationale: Wins on the primary that tank secondary metrics ship net-negative; guardrails prevent that.

rule.statement: The decision MUST be read at the pre-declared sample size or end-date. Early peeking with stop-on-win is rejected unless sequential testing was pre-declared.
rule.rationale: Peeking inflates false-positive rate; stopping on the first p<0.05 is a known statistical failure mode.

rule.statement: The test result MUST emit exactly one of: SHIP | KILL | ITERATE. Soft outcomes ("maybe", "trend positive") are rejected.
rule.rationale: Typed decisions force commitment; soft decisions let the variant ship by default.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
